LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DANIEL RUSH'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. 1) THE AMENDED COMPLAINT INDICATES PURPOSE OF BUSINESS ENTITY WITH MORE PARTICULARITY; 2) ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF BREACH OF CONTRACT AND COMMON COUNTS OF THE STAGE OF THE PROCEEDINGS. COMPLIANCE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, COURT REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

Off Calendar Jun-22-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES.

There being no appearances, parties are deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by a Judge Pro Tem.

THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: Defendants Dan Rush And Daniel Rush'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Response To First Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,790.00 against Plaintiffs jointly and severally. Plaintifs shall pay Defendant Daniel Rush 1,790.00 within 14 days of entry of this order.

MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE PROPOSED ORDER.

Judge Pro Tem Ernst Halperin, not reported.
